Gold stocks rarely appear on growth-oriented scanners; scanner missed record FCF, $2B buyback, and 12.6x Fwd P/E vs. sector 25x — cheapest high-quality gold miner in a $3,200 gold environment
GTF recall overhang kept it off momentum screens even after costs were provisioned Q4 2025; scanner missed the overhang-resolution + dual commercial/defense tailwind combination
High absolute P/E discourages scanner — it misses the FCF yield story; sole-source aerospace parts monopoly with 56% EBITDA margins and 5.2% FCF yield is invisible to earnings-growth screens
Too new as a standalone entity (2023 spinoff) for scanner's historical fundamental screens; parent overhang discount + AI medical imaging opportunity requires spinoff-specific qualitative analysis
Down 35% with negative comps — scanner filters this as a deteriorating trend; misses 12x Fwd P/E, 44M loyalty members, and tariff-overstated narrative that contrarian analysis surfaces
Freight brokerage doesn't fit scanner's growth/catalyst categories; freight cycle recovery requires macro and sector-rotation reading that automated scanners don't perform
Post-crisis with no recent positive catalyst flags; scanner screens on momentum and the 9x Fwd P/E recovery opportunity requires understanding the app-disaster narrative arc
Spinoff from Danaher 2023 — insufficient historical data for fundamental screens; Danaher operating culture quality is a qualitative factor scanner cannot capture
Rare earth materials don't surface in typical screens; the China export restriction + DoD mandatory domestic procurement thesis is a geopolitical signal that leads revenue by 6-12 months

Discovery: The Hormuz crisis is the largest oil supply shock in history, accelerating global energy-security pivots to nuclear; South Korea announced renewed nuclear commitments post-crisis, while AI hyperscaler 20-year direct-supply deals layer onto the thesis — Bank of America targets uranium at $135/lb by 2027 against current ~$90.
value_picks.analyst_take: Nuclear AI data center thesis; US government 50GW nuclear by 2040; Microsoft/Google/Amazon signing uranium supply agreements; locked-in contracts $65+/lb

catalysts_general: Q1 2026 revenue +36% YoY; data center revenue $3.7B; MI300X gaining in HPC and AI inference; Fwd P/E 22x; MI325/MI350 roadmap accelerating. [Tier 4: Bloomberg semiconductor; Tier 1: AMD earnings]

Discovery: Q1 2026 EPS of $1.37 beat estimates of $1.25 by 9.6%; Morgan Stanley raised target to $360 (above consensus $310), indicating potential further upside on AI chip demand recovery after a period of heavy selloff.
value_picks.analyst_take: Data center GPU revenue $3.7B (first time >$3B); 22x Fwd P/E vs. NVDA 25x; MI350 roadmap competitive; NVDA pricing premium creates AMD opportunity in cost-sensitive deployments

catalysts_general: Q1 2026 revenue +47% YoY; hyperscaler connectivity revenue largest segment; Fwd P/E 12x. [Tier 4: Bloomberg tech; Tier 5: Yahoo Finance]

Discovery: Q1 2026 revenue jumped 53% YoY to $4.05B with net earnings tripling to $212M, driven entirely by hyperscaler AI compute and networking demand; company raised full-year 2026 revenue guide by $2B to $19B — a lesser-known EMS play that is the primary assembler of Google TPU racks and liquid-cooled AI systems.
value_picks.analyst_take: 12x Fwd P/E for 47% revenue growth supplying AI hyperscaler custom servers; 400G/800G connectivity largest segment; note: limited upside to fair value — size small

catalysts_general: DoD strategic materials contract $58.5M; GM NdFeB magnet supply agreement; Q1 2026 revenue +35% YoY; tariff near-shoring. [Tier 1: DoD contract announcement; Tier 4: Reuters materials]

Discovery: China's rare earth export control pause expires November 2026, creating an 8-month window of apparent calm that the market is treating as resolved — but the DoD's $110/kg 10-year NdPr price floor and 15% ownership stake represent a structural backstop the market is discounting while the Magnetics Segment just turned EBITDA-positive in Q4 2025.
value_picks.analyst_take: Only US domestic rare earth mine+processor; DoD strategic contract $58.5M; GM supply agreement; tariff-driven near-shoring; China controls 85% of global processing

catalysts_general: Wyoming ISR operations; DoD committed to domestic uranium; AI data center nuclear demand thesis; ISR lowest cost/environmental impact method. [Tier 4: Reuters energy; Tier 1: DoD procurement]

Discovery: A pure-play US-based uranium developer (Wyoming/Texas ISR assets) that avoids the geopolitical exposure of Kazakh or African supply; benefits from the Iran-war energy security pivot AND the nuclear AI-data-center buildout, with smaller market cap meaning higher leverage to uranium price moves than Cameco.
value_picks.analyst_take: All-US uranium ISR producer; DoD domestic uranium preference; AI nuclear energy demand; cheapest way to own nuclear thesis domestically

catalysts_general: Cytisinicline NDA filed; PDUFA H2 2026; Phase 3 showed superior safety to Chantix; $500M+ market if approved. [Tier 1: FDA PDUFA calendar; Tier 3: H.C. Wainwright]

Discovery: PDUFA date June 20, 2026 for cytisinicline — potential first new FDA-approved smoking cessation drug in 20 years; NDA backed by 2,000+ participants in Phase 3 trials with significantly higher abstinence vs placebo.
value_picks.analyst_take: Cytisinicline NDA filed; PDUFA H2 2026; better safety than Chantix (no black box warning) at comparable efficacy; $13 analyst target vs. $4 current; speculative max 0.5% position

catalysts_general: Fwd P/E 30x for 6% growth; Apple Intelligence limited adoption; tariff risk from China manufacturing; iPhone 17 cycle modest.

catalysts_general: Qatar supply disruption temporarily elevated spot LNG prices; European energy independence demand structural but priced in; no near-term catalyst beyond disruption normalization.

Discovery: Iran's attacks on Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG facility (March 2026) triggered force majeure at QatarEnergy, removing ~20% of global LNG supply; Cheniere hit an all-time high on the news and Corpus Christi Stage 4 expansion is DOE-approved, locking in years of contracted European demand at elevated prices.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Qatar LNG supply disruption is temporary catalyst; structural demand from European energy independence and Asian spot market; HOLD until supply disruption resolved into confirmed contract flow

catalysts_general: 4x Fwd P/E; cord-cutting accelerating; NBCUniversal streaming sub-scale vs. Netflix/Disney; broadband penetration saturating.

growth_picks.analyst_take: 4x Fwd P/E appears cheap but cord-cutting is structural decline; no catalyst to re-rate; NBCUniversal streaming not competitive; HOLD until evidence of broadband-only model success

catalysts_general: April 2026 insider activity primarily stock awards, not open-market purchases; Optum CEO Patrick Conway sold shares April 2026; DOJ investigation ongoing. [Tier 1: SEC Form 4 — weaker than claimed]

Discovery: 10 board directors purchased simultaneously on April 1, 2026, with CFO + Optum CEO + UnitedHealthcare CEO also buying on March 17, 2026 — the broadest insider cluster buy event seen in a mega-cap in years, triggered after shares fell ~46% over 12 months; coordinated conviction across both board and operating leadership is historically rare and very bullish.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Discovery insider signal weaker than claimed (stock awards not open-market buys; one Optum CEO sale April 2026); DOJ investigation ongoing; HOLD not BUY

catalysts_general: Revenue growth negative; China expansion under pressure; premium athleisure market showing saturation signals.

Discovery: Shares have lost 62% from their $340 52-week high to ~$129, compressing P/E to 10.5x vs. a decade-long 29-34x historical range — extreme valuation capitulation; Heidi O'Neill (Nike 25-year veteran) named CEO effective September 2026, and Q1 fiscal 2027 earnings are scheduled June 4, 2026 (29 days away) — the first major financial event under the new leadership team that could reset sentiment. Bear case: Americas revenue down 4%, founder Chip Wilson running a proxy opposition campaign, and O'Neill inherits a turnaround-in-progress; new CEO starting Sept means June report still reflects prior leadership with limited guidance visibility.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Revenue growth negative in recent quarters; China execution uncertainty; premium athleisure market saturation; HOLD until earnings confirm North America recovery
